title: US Approves $1B Foreign Military Sale to UK for SSN-AUKUS Submarine Systems
detail: The US Defense Security Cooperation Agency notified Congress on March 20, 2026 of a potential $1 billion Foreign Military Sale to the United Kingdom covering key vertical payload launch system components and weapon launchers for SSN-AUKUS — the next-generation nuclear-powered attack submarine being jointly designed by the US, UK, and Australia under the AUKUS treaty. The package includes vertical deployment tubes (giving the submarine a payload module separate from torpedo tubes), common weapon launchers, software, simulation systems, and embedded technical personnel. The UK government has separately confirmed it plans to acquire up to 12 SSN-AUKUS submarines, with the first expected in the late 2030s; Australia's first domestically-built boat is due in the early 2040s. Analysts noted the sale signals sustained US commitment to the Indo-Pacific submarine architecture despite active Middle East military commitments. Australia faces a coverage gap as its aging Collins-class submarines enter life-of-type extension (LOTE) before Virginia-class transfers begin in the early 2030s.

title: Vietnam Formally Protests China's Antelope Reef Build-Up in Paracel Islands
detail: Vietnam's Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Pham Thu Hang issued a rare formal protest on March 23 over China's large-scale construction at Antelope Reef (Lingyang Jiao) in the Paracel Islands — which Vietnam calls Hoang Sa and claims as sovereign territory. She declared any construction 'without Vietnam's permission is completely illegal and invalid.' Satellite imagery analyzed by CSIS's Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative showed China had reclaimed approximately 1,490 acres at Antelope Reef since October 2025, potentially making it larger than Mischief Reef in the Spratlys. Construction includes over 50 grey-roofed structures, a helipad, jetties, and foundations that may support a 9,000-foot airstrip. China's Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian responded that the Paracels are 'an inherent part of China's territory and there is no dispute about it,' defending the construction as improving 'living conditions' for island residents.

media.caption: Vietnam formally protests China's Antelope Reef build-up in Paracel Islands

title: CSIS AMTI Documents Irreconcilable Philippine-China Interpretations of Second Thomas Shoal Resupply Arrangement
detail: The Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative (CSIS AMTI) published an analysis on March 23 titled 'Divergence and Tacit Understanding in the China-Philippines Provisional Arrangement at Second Thomas Shoal,' documenting that from July 2024 through March 4, 2026, the Philippines carried out 13 RORE (Rotation and Resupply) missions under the provisional arrangement. However, the analysis found that China and the Philippines hold irreconcilably different public interpretations of the agreement: China says Manila accepted prior notification requirements and CCG 'on-site verification' of cargo; the Philippines flatly denies this. The arrangement remains fragile, with China resuming confrontational tactics whenever it judges the Philippines is transporting prohibited materials.
